hero.descripcion: We are the leading network of aquatic physiotherapy professionals in Latin America.

quienes_somos.descripcion:
The Latin American Aquatic Physiotherapy Union (UFAAL) is an organization that integrates professionals, academics, and institutions committed to the development and promotion of aquatic physiotherapy in the Latin American region.

Our purpose is to foster scientific collaboration, professional training, and knowledge exchange, driving the growth of aquatic physiotherapy as an evidence-based therapeutic tool.

UFAAL works to strengthen education, research, and clinical practice in the aquatic medium, promoting Spanish-speaking regional integration and the advancement of physical rehabilitation for the benefit of health and quality of life.

filosofia.contenido:
The Latin American Aquatic Physiotherapy Union (UFAAL) bases its philosophy on the principle that water constitutes a therapeutic medium of high scientific and human value for rehabilitation, prevention, and optimization of physical function.

Our philosophical vision is based on the integration of scientific knowledge, evidence-based clinical practice, and regional cooperation, promoting aquatic physiotherapy developed under principles of professional excellence, ethics, social responsibility, and commitment to the health of populations.

UFAAL conceives the cultural, social, and health diversity of Latin America as a source of enrichment for the exchange of knowledge and the growth of the discipline, fostering academic dialogue, therapeutic innovation, and the continuous training of rehabilitation professionals.

Through collaborative work between countries, academic institutions, and health professionals, the Union drives the strengthening of aquatic physiotherapy as a specialized field of contemporary physiotherapy, aimed at improving functionality, quality of life, and well-being around the Latin American region.

Our philosophy is expressed in the permanent commitment to scientific excellence, ethical practice, and the sustainable development of aquatic physiotherapy, consolidating a professional community that works in an integrated way to advance rehabilitation and health in Latin America.

quienes_somos.mision: Promote the development, strengthening, and positioning of aquatic physiotherapy in Latin America, driving continuous education, scientific research, and ethical professional practice. UFAAL fosters collaboration between professionals, institutions, and organizations in the region, respecting cultural diversity and contributing to the improvement of health, rehabilitation, and quality of life of communities.
quienes_somos.vision: To be a Latin American network of international reference in aquatic physiotherapy, recognized for driving specialized training, scientific research, and evidence-based clinical practice, promoting standards of excellence, social responsibility, and bioethical commitment in the field of rehabilitation in the aquatic medium.

colaboracion_intercultural.descripcion: We promote cooperation between professionals, institutions, and countries in Latin America, valuing cultural diversity and knowledge exchange.

excelencia_academica.descripcion: We drive continuous training, research, and professional updating to ensure therapeutic practices based on scientific evidence.

compromiso_bioetico.descripcion: We act with ethical responsibility, respect for the principles of bioethics, and commitment to the well-being of patients and communities.

innovacion_terapeutica.descripcion: We foster the development of new strategies, methodologies, and technologies applied to aquatic physiotherapy to improve clinical outcomes.

respeto_dignidad.descripcion: We recognize the intrinsic value of each person, promoting patient-centered, inclusive, and humanized care.

transparencia_responsabilidad.descripcion: We promote clear, ethical, and responsible institutional management, based on trust, integrity, and professional commitment.

descripcion: The Latin American Aquatic Physiotherapy Union (UFAAL) arises as a regional initiative aimed at strengthening the integration, representation, and development of aquatic physiotherapy in Latin American countries, particularly in the Spanish-speaking professional community.

The origin of this process is linked to the creation of the International Organisation of Aquatic Physiotherapists (IOAPT), an interest group recognized within World Physiotherapy, whose objective is to promote the advancement of aquatic physiotherapy globally.

Upon learning of the formation of this international organization, Chilean physiotherapist Gloria Aravena identified a historical opportunity for Latin American professionals to actively participate in this space of scientific and professional recognition.

Motivada by this vision, and with the support of the College of Kinesiologists of Chile, she began a regional articulation process in 2020 aimed at convening professionals and representatives from different Latin American countries interested in strengthening the discipline.

Subsequently, in 2023, Gloria Aravena was elected Vice President of the IOAPT, consolidating the region's presence in international leadership alongside leaders such as Karen Morales (Mexico) and Yara Humarán (Puerto Rico).

Today, UFAAL represents a space for meeting and collective growth, where professionals from different countries unite with a common purpose: to advance together from Latin America to the world, strengthening aquatic physiotherapy as a scientific, therapeutic, and human discipline at the service of health and rehabilitation.

formacion.descripcion: At UFAAL, we promote professional development through three fundamental pillars that integrate clinical practice, research, and knowledge dissemination.

eje1.descripcion: We foster the generation of scientific evidence in aquatic physiotherapy, supporting research projects and publishing technical advances that benefit the entire regional community.

eje2.descripcion: We drive continuous training programs, certifications, and university agreements to raise the standard of aquatic physiotherapy in Latin America.

eje3.descripcion: We manage dissemination channels and digital tools that facilitate the exchange of knowledge among experts and access to academic resources.

p1.a: Affiliation is done through the national delegates of each country. You can contact the representative for your region in the 'Member Countries' section or send us an email at ufaal2020@gmail.com requesting information on the specific requirements for your country.

p2.a: Yes, UFAAL, in collaboration with the Training area and certified international teachers, organizes training programs by levels. These trainings are aligned with IOAPT and World Physiotherapy standards.

p3.q: Do you have agreements with universities?
p3.a: We currently work in close collaboration with various leading academic institutions in Latin America to promote research and the inclusion of aquatic physiotherapy in undergraduate and graduate curricula.

p4.q: Who can participate in UFAAL congresses?
p4.a: Our events are open to physiotherapists, kinesiologists, final-year students, and health professionals interested in aquatic rehabilitation. Affiliated members enjoy exclusive benefits and discounts on registration fees.

p5.q: How can I publish a research project in your repository?
p5.a: If you have original research work related to aquatic physiotherapy, you can send it to our Scientific Research Axis for review. Once validated by the committee, it can be published on our digital platform.

s1.p: The Latin American Aquatic Physiotherapy Union (UFAAL) is committed to protecting the privacy and security of the personal information of its affiliates, collaborators, and users of our digital platform. This policy details our data collection and use practices in accordance with international information protection standards.

s2.p: We collect information only when strictly necessary for the fulfillment of our institutional objectives, such as:
items: Contact information (Name, email, phone) voluntarily provided in forms.
Academic and professional information for affiliation or certification processes.
Technical navigation data to improve user experience and site security.

s3.p: The collected data is used exclusively to:
items: Manage institutional communication with member countries.
Inform about training programs, congresses, and scientific advances.
Ensure the correct functioning of the web platform and support services.

s4.p: We implement technical and organizational security measures to prevent unauthorized access, loss, or alteration of your personal information. UFAAL does not sell, rent, or share your data with third parties for commercial purposes.

s5.p: You have the right to access, rectify, or request the deletion of your personal data from our databases at any time. To exercise these rights, you can contact us through our institutional email: ufaal2020@gmail.com

s1.p: By accessing and using this website, you agree to comply with and be bound by the following terms and conditions of use. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, we ask that you do not use our platform.

s2.p: All content present on this site, including but not limited to texts, graphics, logos, images, audios, videos, and scientific documents, is the property of UFAAL or its respective authors and is protected by international copyright and intellectual property laws. Total or partial reproduction without express authorization is prohibited.

s3.p: The material provided on this site is for educational and informational purposes only regarding aquatic physiotherapy. It does not constitute professional medical advice nor substitute personalized clinical evaluation.

s4.p: UFAAL is not responsible for the consequences derived from the improper use of the information contained herein, nor for direct or indirect damages that may arise from accessing our platform or from the impossibility of accessing it.

s5.p: The organization reserves the right to modify these terms and conditions at any time without prior notice. It is the user's responsibility to periodically review this section to be aware of the changes made.
